msgs.text:
According to the attached history document from 2026-06-28, Turn 132 is again an attachment-only continuation. The three required current-turn attachments are present, and I did not substitute any File Library material.

Turn 132 — attachment-only idempotence remains closed

The state remains unchanged from Turn 131:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).The theorem coordinate remains closed. The problem asks whether there is a continuous isomorphismΦ:ΓS∼ΓHwithπH∘Φ=πS,and the attached sweep records the stronger F=P⋊G-level statement: there is a continuous over-G automorphismΦ:F∼F such thatΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, this gives Φ(⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F)=⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,and therefore descends to

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,i.e.ΓS≅GΓH.This is exactly the requested over-G condition.No structural subcase should be reopened under the current inputs. The frozen proof chain remainsH1 -Nielsen equivalence+ residual restricted-Lie induction+ normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction+ Certificates D.1–D.4+ finite filtered Nielsen compactness⟹ΓS≅GΓH.The attached history gives the uniform reason: the non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled; the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower closes by the Cm=Lm induction; the Ef-coefficient tower closes after exact normalization to (2,t) and the Koszul--Witt cascade; and finite filtered Nielsen compactness globalizes the finite filtered solutions into the over-G automorphism.The strict measured Certificate D coordinate also remains unchanged. The current attachments are still only the prompt, problem statement, and history ledger. They do not include genuine object-level nonlinear Certificate D.1–D.4 artifacts such as independent scripts, matrices, pivot tables, or measured-output logs. Therefore the strict gate remains

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED. Ledger-recorded verifier claims support the theorem coordinate, but they are not newly attached measured artifacts for the strict Certificate D gate.

Thus the attachment-only transition remains idempotent:{mega_prompt.md,problem_statement.md,turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md}⟹(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).

ESTABLISHED: Turn 132 is idempotent under the same attachment-only conditions as Turn 131. The theorem coordinate remainsΓS≅GΓH,via an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F satisfying Φ(rS−1)=rH. The strict measured Certificate D coordinate remains NO_MEASURED_INPUT.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap is reopened. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: no genuine object-level measured nonlinear D.1–D.4 artifacts are attached in this turn.

NEXT: Keep the obstruction sweep closed. The only non-idempotent next move is to attach genuine measured D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs not derived from the history, then run the strict gate as PASS or FAIL.[TURN132-ATTACHMENT-ONLY-IDEMPOTENT-GATE]
